rationale: Direct rural pension reform DiD/IV/event-study with fertility outcomes; nonrandom reform exposure and undocumented missing-data handling keep risk moderate.
Flagship pension-extension DiD/cohort exposure design with clear fertility outcomes; residual regional/cohort confounding and missing-data details remain insufficiently documented here.
Pension-reform discontinuity/natural experiment is credible but exposure is expected pension wealth and extraction lacks missing-data detail.
LTCI pilot DiD has fertility outcomes but elder-care margin is not pure pension income; extracted SE is missing and China fertility-policy confounding remains salient.
Historical Prussian social-insurance panel is mechanism-relevant but exposed to ecological confounding, endogenous marriage/fertility margins, and limited missing-data documentation.
Rural China NRPS estimates use DiD/PSM/FE/IV, but participation/rollout selection and overlapping fertility policy create serious residual confounding concerns.
Mechanism evidence only: children instrumented by Later Longer Fewer affect private insurance adoption; this is not an OAS-to-fertility effect estimate.
Historical 23-province pension variation supports the FDT mechanism but regional confounding, aggregate outcome measurement, and sparse missing-data detail raise risk.
Historical county financial-development exposure is well timed for FDT but cross-county design and bank placement selection leave serious confounding risk.
SSA-era social spending is bundled beyond old-age security and sign/mechanism are subtle; cross-state historical design has serious confounding and reporting-selection risk.
